ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pork chop food product entry; no specific entry for marinated pan-fried pork chop with oyster sauce and soy sauce preparation. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers cooked pig meat broadly; no specific code for marinated pan-fried pork chop. Closest parent match used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT code identified for marinated pan-fried pork chop dish. Null used to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of fried pork chop in Singapore is one piece weighing approximately 120 g, commonly served as part of a mixed rice (cai fan) meal or as a standalone protein dish.

nutrient_source.title: Fried Pork Chop — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo database entry for Fried Pork Chop (cr_id F06100014) used directly. All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced from this entry at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat value estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pork loin, pan-fried (NDB 10062) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is documented as an adapted value.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for marinated pan-fried pork chop. GI estimated at 35 based on analogous protein-dominant meat dishes with minimal carbohydrate coating from the University of Sydney GI Database. Carbohydrate content is 3.23 g/100g; glycemic load per 120 g serving = (35 × 3.23 × 1.20) / 100 = 1.36, rounded to 1.4. Low GI classification is consistent with high-protein, low-carbohydrate meat preparations.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 558 mg per 100 g — derived from soy sauce and oyster sauce marinade — exceeds the 400 mg threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 80–100 g and request reduced-sodium marinade preparation; pairing with potassium-rich vegetables such as spinach or broccoli can partially offset sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate content is only 3.23 g per 100 g with 0 g sugar; high protein at 24.09 g per 100 g supports glycaemic stability and slows gastric emptying.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Fried pork chop is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without sweet dipping sauces or glazes; the low carbohydrate and high protein profile supports postprandial blood glucose control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Pan-frying adds fat and the soy-oyster marinade contributes 558 mg sodium per 100 g; saturated fat estimated at approximately 2 g per 100 g from lean pork loin.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit sodium intake from this dish by requesting reduced-soy marinade, choose grilled or baked preparation over pan-frying, and keep portion size to one piece (approximately 100–120 g) to manage saturated fat and sodium contribution.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid) that can amplify inflammatory cytokines; pan-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); sodium 558 mg per 100 g exceeds the 400 mg RA threshold.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute pork with chicken breast or oily fish, request grilled or baked preparation to minimise AGE formation, and avoid additional soy or chilli sauce at the table.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried Pork Chop (炸猪排; Zha Zhu Pai) is a popular Singaporean hawker and cai fan (mixed rice) dish consisting of a lean pork chop marinated in oyster sauce, soy sauce, and spices, then pan-fried until golden and cooked through. The result is a savoury, slightly caramelised exterior with a tender, juicy interior. The marinade imparts umami depth from the oyster and soy sauces, with aromatic spice notes from black pepper and garlic. Per 100 g, the dish provides 161 kcal, 24.09 g protein, 5.77 g fat, and only 3.23 g carbohydrate with 0 g sugar, making it a high-protein, low-carbohydrate option. Sodium is elevated at 558 mg per 100 g due to the soy and oyster sauce marinade, which is a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic impact is minimal given the negligible carbohydrate content, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without sweet sauces. It is commonly served as a protein component in a mixed rice meal alongside steamed rice and vegetables. The dish is not suitable for halal diets due to pork content, and contains soy and shellfish allergens from the marinade.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a fried pork chop?
Is fried pork chop suitable for diabetics?
Can I eat fried pork chop if I have high blood pressure?
Is pork chop high in protein?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Fried Pork Chop (cr_id F06100014) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pork loin pan-fried entry as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for protein-dominant meat dishes. All other fields are consistent with the HPB source and approved secondary references.
notes: sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — the marinade ingredients (oyster sauce, soy sauce, spices) contribute negligible sugar at the reported carbohydrate level of 3.23 g/100g. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific marinated pan-fried pork chop preparation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for F06100014, and no sufficiently close match was found in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy to provide these values with confidence. The input dietary_tags included 'plant_based' which is incorrect for a pork dish and has been removed. The 'keto' tag has been retained as a dietary flag consideration given the low carbohydrate content. Saturated fat of 2.0 g/100g is an adapted estimate from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 10062 (pork loin, pan-fried) and should be verified against HPB if fatty acid data becomes available.
